author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text:
Implementation complete:
- Created API/QueryStream.swift with AsyncSequence conformance
- QueryStream wraps AsyncThrowingStream<ResponseChunk, Error>
- Caches InitSystemMessage when encountered during iteration
- Thread-safe with NSLock
- All 6 unit tests passing: iteration, async iterator protocol, init message caching, pre-cached init message, error propagation, empty stream

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text:
Task complete:
- Changed .stream case from AnyPublisher to QueryStream
- Added temporary bridge initializer QueryStream(bridging:) for backward compat
- Made ResponseChunk @unchecked Sendable for Swift 6 concurrency
- Updated backends to use bridge (with TODO for full migration in 1.3/1.4)
- Added testClaudeCodeResultStreamCase test
- All 7 QueryStream tests passing, build successful

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text: Starting migration - replacing PassthroughSubject with AsyncThrowingStream.Continuation

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text:
Migration complete:
- Removed Combine import and cancellables
- Replaced PassthroughSubject with AsyncThrowingStream.Continuation
- Updated handleStreamJsonOutput() to use makeStream()
- Replaced subject.send() with continuation.yield()
- Returns QueryStream directly (no bridge)
- Added test, all tests passing

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text:
Migration complete - same pattern as AgentSDKBackend:
- Removed Combine import and cancellables
- Updated handleStreamJsonOutput() to use AsyncThrowingStream
- Updated processJsonLine() and processSystemMessage() to use continuation
- Returns QueryStream directly
- Added test, all 21 tests passing

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text: JSONRPC types already implemented in ControlMessages.swift: JSONRPCMessage (serves as both request/response), JSONRPCError with standard error codes. MCPServerError enum added in SDKMCPServer.swift. Tests exist in ControlMessagesTests.swift.

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text: HookTypes.swift compiles successfully. Creating unit tests.

author: Yaakov M Nemoy
text: 7.3 requires modifying the original ClaudeCodeSDK package which has a module naming conflict with NativeClaudeCodeSDK (both named 'ClaudeCodeSDK'). This integration task should be done post-bakeoff when merging/replacing implementations. The NativeClaudeCodeSDK is feature-complete as a standalone SDK.
